source_pr.title: executor: Fix group_concat returns wrong results after set group_concat_max_len
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #27751

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Use `heap.pop` instead of `rows[0]` to get the top element of the heap in `topNRows::tryToAdd`

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Fix the issue that group_concat returns wrong results after set group_concat_max_len
```

symptoms: Deterministic wrong result: `GROUP_CONCAT` returns incorrect output after setting `group_concat_max_len` (Issue #27751).
failure_signature: Wrong result: GROUP_CONCAT output mismatch after `set @@group_concat_max_len=0` (Issue #27751).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes a deterministic correctness bug in `GROUP_CONCAT` truncation logic (using `heap.Pop` instead of directly indexing the heap array) and adds a regression unit test for Issue #27751. The patch does not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization fix for an intermittent failure, so keep this case as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: Fix GROUP_CONCAT truncation: use `heap.Pop` to get the correct heap top row, and add regression test for Issue #27751.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select group_concat(nname order by 1 separator '#' ) from t;").Check(testkit.Rows("11#1")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)

analysis:

PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/35852
Triage sources: manual_triage, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: tk.MustQuery("select group_concat(nname order by 1 separator '#' ) from t;").Check(testkit.Rows("11#1")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
Changed files (2): executor/aggfuncs/func_group_concat.go, executor/aggregate_test.go
Patch url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/35852.patch
Picked test: type=unit path=executor/aggregate_test.go name=aggregate_test
